abstract: This review summarizes the complex pathological microenvironment of refractory wounds, tissue-regenerative mechanisms of stem cell-derived nanovesicles (SC-NVs), smart hydrogel-based delivery systems, and clinical challenges and prospects.

title: Impact of 2′-fluoro nucleobase modifications on CD detection, sensitivity and specificity of short oligonucleotides bound to alginate hydrogels
abstract: We discuss the synthesis, characterization, and analysis of label-free, short oligonucleotide-bound alginate hydrogels and improved function with 2′-fluoro nucleobase modification.

title: Multifunctional pyrazino[2,3- g ]quinoxaline derivative capable of acidochromic response, cancer cell imaging, and as an active layer for organic light-emitting diodes
abstract: Pyrazino[2,3- g ]quinoxaline derivative capable of acid sensing, cancer cell imaging and usable as OLED.

title: Natural polyphenol-taurine-based nanoparticles for oral ulcer therapy
abstract: We report a one-step oxidative copolymerization to fabricate pH/ROS dual-responsive PAT NPs. It exhibits good biocompatibility, free radical scavenging, anti-inflammation, macrophage regulation, and pro-angiogenesis. In vivo , PAT NPs suppress inflammation and accelerate oral ulcer healing.

title: A–D–A–D–A structured organic semiconductor liposomes for NIR-II fluorescence imaging and surgical navigation of lymph node metastasis
abstract: Fluorescence-guided surgery enables precise intraoperative “mapping” of malignant tissue, allowing surgeons to identify and excise small or occult lesions while preserving healthy tissues, reducing recurrence/metastasis risks and promoting recovery.

title: Advances in therapeutic strategies for atherosclerosis: from pharmacologics to stents and stent coatings
abstract: A spectrum of anti-atherosclerotic approaches aimed at restoring vasculature, from pharmacological agents to recent advances in stent coatings.

title: Molecular engineering of aggregation-induced emission enhanced photosensitizers to boost the theranostic performance in photodynamic therapy
abstract: Schematic illustration of TPEPyTMB-3 nanoparticle fabrication for mitochondria targeting photodynamic therapy in a hypoxic environment.

title: Recent advances in carbohydrate polymer-supported composites for monitoring breast cancer biomarkers: a comprehensive review
abstract: This paper reviews breast cancer (BC) impacts, biomarker categories, and advances in related sensors using carbohydrate polymer composites for BC detection, including strategies to enhance sensing efficacy, challenges, and future prospects.

title: In situ hydrothermal synthesis of a zeolite–PVA hemostatic sponge and its rapid hemostasis in a lethal massive hemorrhage model
abstract: In situ hydrothermally synthesized zeolite–PVA sponge for rapid hemostasis in lethal massive hemorrhage.

title: Particle accumulation and medullary expansion associated with LA103Z magnesium alloy degradation in a rat intramedullary model
abstract: Mg alloy degradation in rat medulla releases micron particles, inducing M1 macrophage polarization and proinflammatory cytokines, which activate osteoclasts and cause endosteal resorption, medullary expansion, and cortical thinning.

title: Recent advances in non-vascular stents for occlusive luminal disease treatment
abstract: This review highlights the evolution of stent therapy in treating various non-vascular occlusive luminal diseases, moving from stents currently used in standard clinical practices to the next generation of biodegradable and drug-eluting stents.

title: Granular, hierarchically porous polymer scaffolds for bone tissue engineering
abstract: Bone fractures remain a significant clinical and economic burden. This work describes the preparation and preliminary evaluation of a granular, porous microparticle scaffold for bone regeneration.

title: Supramolecular adenosine-based skin delivery system for hair regulation and restoration
abstract: We report TA–Ado, an adenosine-tartaric acid cocrystal. It enhances the transdermal delivery of adenosine and exhibits synergistic bioactivities. Clinical data confirm its safety and efficacy for scalp care.

title: Stress relaxation timescale and hydrogel network connectivity regulate neural progenitor cell stemness and differentiation
abstract: A family of modular recombinant protein–synthetic polymer hybrid hydrogels reveals that stress relaxation timescale and network connectivity regulate neural progenitor cell stemmness.

title: Biological effect of silver nanowires in biomedical applications
abstract: Silver nanowires show significant promise in diverse biomedical applications including orthopedics, skin wound repair, tumor therapy, wearable devices, skeletal muscle, and nerve repair.

title: Photothermally triggered silk fibroin microneedles with coordinated gallic acid–iron networks for synergistic infected burn wound therapy
abstract: Silk fibroin microneedles with gallic acid-iron networks were made for infected burns. NIR-triggered photothermal antibacterial action and sequential bioactive release provide synergistic antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and regenerative effects.

title: Eggshell-derived nanoparticles accelerate wound healing
abstract: Overall scheme demonstrating the wound healing property of eggshell-derived nanoparticles.

title: Intelligent catalase-coated MnO 2 nanoparticles with programmed oxygen supply and glutathione depletion for enhanced photodynamic therapy
abstract: Catalase-coated MnO 2 nanoparticles enable facile drug loading via physical adsorption, while concurrently alleviating tumor hypoxia and scavenging glutathione, leading to highly effective treatment of solid tumors.

title: Radiopaque coating for improved implantability and in vivo imaging of neural probes
abstract: Flexible neural probes coated with radiodense hydrogels enable non-invasive μCT visualization and facilitate insertion, while providing a soft interface post-implantation.

title: Ionizable mesoporous silica nanoparticle-mediated siAURKB delivery for colorectal cancer therapy
abstract: An ionizable mesoporous silica nanoparticle (MSN) for siRNA delivery was developed by modifying diethylamino groups onto the surface. After loading with Aurora-B siRNA, the formulated nanomedicine realized effective colorectal cancer therapy.
